<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-de-checkpoint" data-default-total="4"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "first-order-equations";
    const BANKS = {
      "first-order-equations": [odeMeaningQuestion, solutionQuestion, initialConditionQuestion],
      "separable-equations": [separableClassQuestion, separationStepQuestion, constantQuestion],
      "linear-equations": [linearFormQuestion, integratingFactorQuestion, productDerivativeQuestion],
      "slope-fields": [slopeSegmentQuestion, eulerStepQuestion, equilibriumStabilityQuestion],
      systems: [systemEquilibriumQuestion, couplingQuestion, phasePlaneQuestion],
      "differential-equations-cumulative-review": [
        odeMeaningQuestion,
        separableClassQuestion,
        integratingFactorQuestion,
        eulerStepQuestion,
        systemEquilibriumQuestion,
      ],
    };

    document.querySelectorAll(".edu-math-de-checkpoint:not([data-ready])").forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-de-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Differential equations checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 4))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", {
        class: "quiz-count",
        type: "number",
        min: "1",
        max: "20",
        value: String(state.total),
      });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 4));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element("div", { class: "quiz-start" }, element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input), button),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(
          correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`,
          " ",
          question.explanation,
        );
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function odeMeaningQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does a first-order ODE usually describe?",
        stimulus: "dy/dt = f(t, y)",
        answer: "a rule for the rate of change",
        distractors: ["only one number", "an area formula", "a matrix inverse"],
        explanation: "The derivative tells how the unknown function changes.",
      });
    }

    function solutionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which function solves y' = 2y?",
        stimulus: "Differentiate each candidate mentally.",
        answer: "Ce^(2x)",
        distractors: ["2x + C", "C e^x", "x^2 + C"],
        explanation: "The derivative of Ce^(2x) is 2Ce^(2x), which equals 2y.",
      });
    }

    function initialConditionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does y(0)=5 do?",
        stimulus: "Family: y = C e^x",
        answer: "selects one solution curve",
        distractors: ["changes the derivative rule", "removes x from the problem", "proves no solution exists"],
        explanation: "The initial condition determines the constant C.",
      });
    }

    function separableClassQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Is this equation separable?",
        stimulus: "dy/dx = x y",
        answer: "yes",
        distractors: ["no", "only if x=0", "only after differentiating twice"],
        explanation: "It can be written as (1/y) dy = x dx when y is not zero.",
      });
    }

    function separationStepQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the separated form of y' = x y²?",
        stimulus: "Move y terms with dy and x terms with dx.",
        answer: "y^(-2) dy = x dx",
        distractors: ["y² dy = x dx", "dy = x y² dx", "x dy = y² dx"],
        explanation: "Divide both sides by y², then multiply by dx.",
      });
    }

    function constantQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Why does +C appear after integrating?",
        stimulus: "∫ y^(-1) dy = ∫ x dx",
        answer: "indefinite integrals represent families",
        distractors: ["C means cancel", "C is always zero", "C is the derivative"],
        explanation: "One differential equation can have many solution curves.",
      });
    }

    function linearFormQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Identify p(x) in y' + 3y = x.",
        stimulus: "Standard form: y' + p(x)y = q(x)",
        answer: "3",
        distractors: ["x", "y", "1/3"],
        explanation: "p(x) is the coefficient multiplying y in standard form.",
      });
    }

    function integratingFactorQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the integrating factor for y' + 2y = x?",
        stimulus: "mu(x) = e^(∫p(x)dx)",
        answer: "e^(2x)",
        distractors: ["e^x", "2e^x", "x²/2"],
        explanation: "Here p(x)=2, so the integral is 2x.",
      });
    }

    function productDerivativeQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "After multiplying by mu, what should the left side become?",
        stimulus: "linear ODE workflow",
        answer: "the derivative of mu(x)y",
        distractors: ["a second derivative", "zero", "a separated fraction"],
        explanation: "That product derivative is why the integrating factor helps.",
      });
    }

    function slopeSegmentQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does one short segment in a slope field show?",
        stimulus: "At a point (x,y)",
        answer: "the local slope predicted by the ODE",
        distractors: ["the final answer", "the x-intercept", "the area under a curve"],
        explanation: "The segment visualizes dy/dx at that point.",
      });
    }

    function eulerStepQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Use one Euler step for y'=y, y(0)=2, h=0.5. What is y1?",
        stimulus: "y1 = y0 + h f(x0,y0)",
        answer: "3",
        distractors: ["2.5", "1", "4"],
        explanation: "The current slope is 2, so y1 = 2 + 0.5·2 = 3.",
      });
    }

    function equilibriumStabilityQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which value is an equilibrium for y'=y(1-y)?",
        stimulus: "Set the right-hand side equal to zero.",
        answer: "1",
        distractors: ["2", "-1", "1/2"],
        explanation: "Both y=0 and y=1 are equilibria; 1 is one of them.",
      });
    }

    function systemEquilibriumQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Find the equilibrium of x'=2-x, y'=3-y.",
        stimulus: "Set both derivatives to zero.",
        answer: "(2, 3)",
        distractors: ["(0, 0)", "(-2, -3)", "(3, 2)"],
        explanation: "Solving 2-x=0 and 3-y=0 gives (2,3).",
      });
    }

    function couplingQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does coupling mean in a system?",
        stimulus: "x' may depend on y and y' may depend on x",
        answer: "variables influence each other's rates",
        distractors: ["variables are constant", "only one equation is used", "all derivatives are zero"],
        explanation: "Coupled variables interact through the derivative rules.",
      });
    }

    function phasePlaneQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "In a phase plane, what does an arrow represent?",
        stimulus: "State point (x,y)",
        answer: "the velocity vector (x', y')",
        distractors: ["only the value of y", "an antiderivative", "a probability"],
        explanation: "The arrow shows how the state moves from that point.",
      });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return {
        prompt,
        stimulus,
        answer,
        explanation,
        options: shuffle(unique([answer, ...distractors])).slice(0, 4),
      };
    }

    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const form = element("form", { class: "quiz-options", role: "radiogroup" });
      options.forEach((option) => {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name: answerName(), value: option });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        form.append(element("label", { class: "quiz-option" }, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      });
      return form;
    }

    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("div", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, element("div", { class: "quiz-math" }, text));
    }

    function choice(items) {
      return items[Math.floor(Math.random() * items.length)];
    }

    function shuffle(items) {
      return [...items].sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5);
    }

    function unique(items) {
      return [...new Set(items)];
    }

    function answerName() {
      return `de-answer-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
    }

    function element(tag, attrs = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([key, value]) => {
        if (value === false || value === null || value === undefined) return;
        if (key === "class") node.className = value;
        else node.setAttribute(key, String(value));
      });
      children.flat().forEach((child) => node.append(child));
      return node;
    }
  })();
</script>
